pubmed_links: [Tsai et al. (2020) Pathogenic Variants in CEP85L Cause Sporadic and Familial Posterior Predominant Lissencephaly](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32097630/); [Kodani et al. (2020) Posterior Neocortex-Specific Regulation of Neuronal Migration by CEP85L Identifies Maternal Centriole-Dependent Activation of CDK5](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32097629/); [van Hulzen et al. (2017) Genetic Overlap Between Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder and Bipolar Disorder: Evidence From Genome-wide Association Study Meta-analysis](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27890468/)
summary: CEP85L encodes a centrosomal protein required for neuronal migration; rare pathogenic variants cause lissencephaly type 10 (OMIM #617609) with intellectual disability, developmental delay, and epilepsy in ~70-90% of affected individuals, acting via the CEP85L-CDK5-LIS1 pathway. A common intronic variant (rs11756438) reached genome-wide significance in a cross-disorder GWAS for shared ADHD/bipolar disorder risk, and has been followed up in schizophrenia association studies. (well-established)
